Politics.headline: Israel and Lebanon say extending ceasefire despite new strikes
Politics.detail: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend a ceasefire and hold expanded talks on a political settlement, the United States announced Friday, even as Israel carried out new strikes that it insists are not subject to the truce. Israel has been pounding Lebanon and invaded its south in response to fire from Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed Shia movement that is not part of the ceasefire diplomacy.

Politics.headline: '20 years is enough'; Trump puts a timeline on limiting Iran's nuclear program
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump said a 20-year moratorium on Iran's nuclear program would be enough for him to strike a deal and end the war. The allowance for Iran to enrich uranium at any point in the future — even decades out — marks a shift for the president, who has repeatedly insisted that the country never be allowed to do so.

Politics.headline: '$30 for a hamburger': Trump's facing no good options on beef prices
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump returned home from China to a politically-fraught domestic policy challenge: sky-high beef prices. The White House must now decide how to proceed with an executive order to temporarily ease tariffs on imported beef — a move that was delayed earlier this week amid intense backlash from farm-state lawmakers and industry allies.

Politics.headline: Trump-Xi Summit kicks off exposing different world views but generally on script
Politics.detail: Donald Trump opened his Beijing visit by casting relations with Xi Jinping in personal terms, calling him “a great leader” and stressing that the two men could resolve problems directly. Xi used the same encounter to set limits, warning that the United States must handle Taiwan with “utmost caution” and describing it as the most critical issue in the relationship.

Politics.headline: Carney, Smith reach energy agreement that could see pipeline construction start in 2027
Politics.detail: The federal and Alberta governments have advanced a climate and energy agreement that could see construction on an oil pipeline to the West Coast start as early as September 2027. Alberta premier says investors view 'aggressive timelines' as a sign that Alberta and Canada are serious about a pipeline.

Finance & Economy.headline: Pound heads for worst week in 18 months as Burnham lines up Labour bid
Finance & Economy.detail: UK government borrowing costs jump amid political uncertainty and oil price rise that fuelled inflation worries, contributing to the pound's decline.

Finance & Economy.headline: India's April exports jump 13.8%, trade gap widens to 3-month high
Finance & Economy.detail: India's exports saw a significant increase in April, but a widening trade gap indicates imports are growing at an even faster pace.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks fall on Iran war fears; Treasury yields jump
Finance & Economy.detail: US stocks declined as investors are worried about the impact of the conflict with Iran, particularly regarding higher energy prices potentially fueling inflation, while the 10-year Treasury note yield rose to a one-year high.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's policies are working together to hike prices, as midterms approach
Finance & Economy.detail: The confluence of former President Trump's economic policies is contributing to rising prices as the midterm elections draw closer.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Industrial Output Growth at 14-Month High
Finance & Economy.detail: Industrial production in the United States increased 0.7% in April 2026, marking the largest gain in 14 months, exceeding market expectations with a rise in manufacturing and utilities output.

Technology.headline: Maris-Tech Announces Full-Year 2025 Financial Results and Continued Strategic Focus on AI-Powered Edge Video Solutions
Technology.detail: Maris-Tech, a global leader in video and AI-based edge computing technology, announced its 2025 financial results, highlighting continued investment in compact, low-power video streaming and edge AI platforms for defense and homeland security applications and a financing transaction in March 2026 to support these initiatives. The company remains focused on advancing AI video and edge computing solutions for mission-critical applications.

Technology.headline: Tiny Tech Fuels Big Advances in Cancer Detection
Technology.detail: The Marble Center for Cancer Nanomedicine at MIT is seeing its vision materialize in publications, community startups, and patients' lives being changed through nanomedicine. The center also announced a new grant in June aimed at advancing new imaging and sensing technologies for precision medicine.

Technology.headline: Ackman, Loeb take different routes on tech bets in early 2026
Technology.detail: Bill Ackman and Daniel Loeb's investment strategies in tech companies have diverged in early 2026, reflecting different perspectives on the sector's potential. This highlights the varied approaches investors are taking in the dynamic technology market.

Technology.headline: Silicon Valley Wants to Put a Chip in Your Brain
Technology.detail: Silicon Valley is increasingly interested in brain-computer interfaces, with some envisioning a future where humans merge with AI.  Despite the early stage of the technology, significant investments are being made, raising debates about privacy and the future of humanity.

Technology.headline: Microsoft adds more former Ai2 researchers, bolstering its Superintelligence team
Technology.detail: Microsoft is strengthening its Superintelligence team by hiring at least 10 former Allen Institute for AI (Ai2) researchers and staffers, including the core members of Ai2's OLMo open-source model effort. This move comes as Ai2 shifts its funding focus towards applied AI applications.

Science.headline: Giant New Dinosaur Species Discovered in Thailand Reveals Hidden Diversity of Asian Titans
Science.detail: A massive new dinosaur discovered in Thailand, named Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis, is rewriting Southeast Asia's prehistoric history as a colossal long-necked sauropod weighing around 27 tons. This discovery highlights the hidden diversity of Asian Titanosaurs.

Science.headline: Homo erectus May Have Co-Existed with Denisovans in East Asia
Science.detail: Recent findings suggest that Homo erectus may have co-existed with Denisovans in East Asia, adding a new layer to the understanding of human evolution in the region. This discovery challenges previous assumptions about the timeline and interactions of different hominin species.

Science.headline: Implantable bacteria can now be safely contained, clearing a major hurdle for fighting infection and cancer
Science.detail: Scientists have developed a method to safely contain implantable bacteria, marking a significant advancement in using bacteria to combat infections and cancer. This new approach addresses a major safety concern, paving the way for innovative treatments.

Science.headline: Neuron imaging captures unconventional receptor route that supports synaptic communication.
Science.detail: New neuron imaging techniques have revealed an unconventional receptor route that plays a critical role in supporting synaptic communication. This discovery enhances the understanding of how neurons transmit signals, potentially leading to new insights into neurological disorders.

Science.headline: Scientists Say Just 30 Minutes of Exercise a Week Could Transform Your Health
Science.detail: Researchers have found that just 30 minutes of high-intensity exercise per week, broken into small bursts, can significantly improve health. This suggests that even minimal exercise can have substantial benefits.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover Vitamin B2 May Help Cancer Cells Survive
Health.detail: Scientists have uncovered a surprising dark side to vitamin B2: it may help cancer cells stay alive by supporting a cellular shield that protects tumors from ferroptosis, a form of programmed cell death linked to cancer suppression. The discovery was made in the lab.

Health.headline: The Brain's “Feel Good” Chemical May Be Secretly Fueling Tinnitus
Health.detail: Scientists have uncovered evidence that serotonin, the same brain chemical boosted by many antidepressants, may actually worsen tinnitus. Using advanced light-based brain stimulation in mice, researchers identified a serotonin-driven circuit.

Health.headline: One in Five People May Carry This Hidden Cholesterol Risk Without Knowing It
Health.detail: Researchers analyzing over 20,000 patients found that very high levels of the inherited cholesterol particle Lp(a) dramatically raise the risk of stroke, cardiovascular death, and major heart.

Health.headline: Menopause Symptoms Worse in Women With Abdominal Obesity
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that women with abdominal obesity experience more severe menopause symptoms. The research highlights the connection between body fat distribution and menopausal health.

Health.headline: Massachusetts man speaks again after pioneering larynx transplant
Health.detail: A Massachusetts man can speak again after surgeons removed his cancerous voice box and replaced it with a donated one, a pioneering move. Marty Kedian is only the third patient in the U.S. ever to undergo a larynx transplant — the first with active cancer — and one of a handful worldwide.

Sports.headline: Ollie Watkins punishes Liverpool, as Aston Villa seal Champions League place
Sports.detail: Champions League hopefuls Liverpool remain vulnerable to being caught by both Bournemouth and Brighton after Ollie Watkins led Aston Villa to a 4-2 victory. Aston Villa secured Champions League qualification at Liverpool's expense.

Sports.headline: Jets sign veteran wide receiver Tim Patrick, waive kicker Will Ferrin
Sports.detail: The New York Jets have signed veteran wide receiver Tim Patrick and waived kicker Will Ferrin. This move comes as the Jets prepare for the upcoming NFL season.

Sports.headline: FIFA announces Super Bowl-style World Cup final halftime show featuring Madonna, Shakira and BTS
Sports.detail: FIFA has announced that the World Cup final will feature a Super Bowl-style halftime show with performances by Madonna, Shakira, and BTS. The event aims to enhance the fan experience and celebrate the global appeal of the World Cup.

Sports.headline: Golden Knights docked 2nd-round pick, John Tortorella fined $100K for breaking media rules
Sports.detail: The Golden Knights have been penalized with a second-round draft pick deduction, and coach John Tortorella has been fined $100,000 for violating media regulations. The specific details of the infraction were not immediately disclosed.

Sports.headline: Keaon Koloamatangi pushing for NSW recall, backs Campbell Graham to play for the Blues
Sports.detail: Keaon Koloamatangi is in contention for a recall to the NSW Blues State of Origin team, with coach Laurie Daley expected to name a new front-row pairing due to injuries. Koloamatangi has been a standout performer for the South Sydney Rabbitohs and aims to seize the opportunity if selected.

Entertainment.headline: Drake Shocks Internet With Triple Album Release
Entertainment.detail: Drake surprised fans by releasing not one, but three new albums: 'Iceman,' 'Habibti,' and 'Maid of Honour' all in one day. Fans around the world were treated to the triple release.

Entertainment.headline: Judge declares a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial.
Entertainment.detail: A judge has declared a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial. The retrial has been closely followed by the entertainment industry.

Entertainment.headline: Shakira and Burna Boy release official 2026 FIFA World Cup Anthem, 'Dai Dai'
Entertainment.detail: Shakira and Burna Boy have collaborated to release 'Dai Dai,' the official anthem for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The song is expected to be a global hit.

Entertainment.headline: Pete Davidson and Elsie Hewitt 'are working on things'
Entertainment.detail: Pete Davidson and Elsie Hewitt are reportedly trying to reconcile. The couple had previously split five months after welcoming their daughter.

Entertainment.headline: Prince William holds key decision until Kate Middleton's return from Italy
Entertainment.detail: Prince William is waiting for Kate Middleton's return from her solo trip to Italy to make a key decision. Kate's visit was described as a significant moment after her cancer recovery.

Crime & Justice.headline: Iraqi accused of terrorism attacks and plots in US and Europe arrested and charged
Crime & Justice.detail: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood al-Saadi appeared in US federal court to face six terrorism-related charges related to planning attacks in the U.S. and elsewhere.

Crime & Justice.headline: Rise in deed theft across US prompts crackdown in New York City
Crime & Justice.detail: Authorities in New York City are cracking down on deed theft, which is on the rise across the United States.

Crime & Justice.headline: Terror plot foiled, suspect under arrest
Crime & Justice.detail: A terror plot targeting Jewish institutions in New York, California and Arizona has been foiled, and a man linked to the alleged scheme is under arrest; prosecutors say the Iraqi suspect is tied to other global attacks and claim he wanted payback for the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran.

Crime & Justice.headline: Iraqi man charged with terror plot: officials
Crime & Justice.detail: An Iraqi national was arrested and charged with planning to carry out several terror attacks in the U.S., including at a New York City synagogue and other prominent Jewish centers in Los Angeles and Scottsdale, Arizona.

Crime & Justice.headline: Sources: Iraqi national planned synagogue attacks
Crime & Justice.detail: Federal prosecutors charged an Iraqi national on Friday with planning several terrorist attacks in the U.S. as retribution for the Iran war.

Education.headline: Halifax school librarian, social worker positions up in the air after changes to funding
Education.detail: Councillors recently voted to ratify a new five-year supplementary education funding agreement this week, with the funds prioritizing music and fine arts in schools, potentially impacting librarian and social worker roles.

Education.headline: EEOC moves to axe EEO-5 reporting
Education.detail: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) is taking steps to eliminate the EEO-5 reporting requirements, which impacts data collection on staff in public elementary and secondary schools.

Education.headline: King's College London to merge with Cranfield University
Education.detail: King's College London and Cranfield University announced a surprise merger, hoping to create a stronger institution together.

Education.headline: Ed Department to release $144M for special education, early intervention
Education.detail: The U.S. Education Department will release $144 million for special education and early intervention programs, and has issued guidance to states on using IDEA funds to support expectant parents of children with disabilities.

Education.headline: Interest in Career and Tech Ed. Has Jumped. Which Fields Will See the Biggest Growth?
Education.detail: An EdWeek Research Center survey indicates a growing student interest in career-focused courses, reflecting shifts in workforce readiness priorities.

Lifestyle.headline: Madhuri Dixit's Secret To Glowing Skin At 59: Cleanser, Toner, And Sunscreen
Lifestyle.detail: Madhuri Dixit shares her skincare routine emphasizing the importance of cleanser, toner, and sunscreen for maintaining glowing skin at 59.

Lifestyle.headline: Rivals actor Katherine Parkinson lauds series for depicting women's view of sex
Lifestyle.detail: Katherine Parkinson praises the series Rivals for its portrayal of women's perspectives on sex.

Lifestyle.headline: Anderson juices up the vibes for Dior with spotlight on Hollywood.
Lifestyle.detail: Anderson highlighted Hollywood in the Dior collection.

Lifestyle.headline: Models welcome a new wave of maturity on Australian fashion week runways.
Lifestyle.detail: Australian Fashion Week runways are seeing a rise in mature models.

Lifestyle.headline: Mschf designs kit, capsule for OTB-backed soccer club L.R. Vicenza
Lifestyle.detail: Mschf has created a kit and capsule collection for the soccer club L.R. Vicenza, which is supported by OTB.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: People can write a new chapter in Sino-US exchanges
Opinion/Editorial.detail: People-to-people exchanges have consistently acted as a stabilizing force, fostering mutual understanding and facilitating progress across various domains, which is especially important during times of political distrust.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Dangerous days lie ahead after the outbreak of hantavirus
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Following a hantavirus outbreak, those returning to their home countries must be supported logistically, medically, and emotionally to quarantine for the full WHO-recommended 42 days to prevent further infections, with the WHO taking the lead in response due to the US's reduced role.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Supporting frontline healthcare
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taiwan is implementing mandated shift nurse-to-patient ratios in phases starting May 20 next year, earlier than planned, to address excessive workloads, with hospitals facing fines for non-compliance; however, this is only a partial solution to the nation's demographic and healthcare workforce challenges.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Doubts over graft poll
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece in the Bangkok Post discusses concerns and uncertainties surrounding an upcoming election related to corruption.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Camp Mystic remains closed, but is the Texas Hill Country prepared for flooding?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial in the San Antonio Express-News discusses the continued closure of Camp Mystic and questions the preparedness of the Texas Hill Country for potential flooding events.

World.headline: Ukraine attacks Russia with drones after suffering three days of massive strikes
World.detail: Following a series of intense strikes on Kyiv, Ukraine launched retaliatory drone attacks against Russia, escalating the conflict between the two nations. The recent exchanges highlight the ongoing volatility and widening scope of the war.

World.headline: Trump leaves China without breakthroughs on Iran, Taiwan or AI
World.detail: Donald Trump concluded his visit to China without achieving significant progress on key issues such as Iran, Taiwan, and artificial intelligence. Despite the lack of breakthroughs, the summit served as a platform for discussions on critical geopolitical matters.

World.headline: Shaky truce between Israel and Lebanon extended for 45 days, US says
World.detail: The United States has announced an extension of the fragile truce between Israel and Lebanon for another 45 days following productive talks in Washington. This extension aims to provide a window for further negotiations and de-escalation of tensions in the region.

World.headline: Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo
World.detail: A recent Ebola outbreak in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has resulted in 65 fatalities, raising concerns among health organizations. Efforts are being intensified to contain the spread of the virus and provide medical assistance to those affected.

World.headline: German leader Merz says he 'would not advise my children to go' to US
World.detail: Friedrich Merz, a German leader, has expressed reservations about the United States, stating that he would not recommend his children to live there. His comments reflect concerns about the current social and political climate in the U.S.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabweans lured into Russia's war as families plead for their return
Zimbabwe.detail: Families are pleading for the return of Zimbabweans who have been lured into fighting in the Russia-Ukraine war.

Zimbabwe.headline: Dynamos not strong enough – admits Mangombe after Herentals draw extends winless streak
Zimbabwe.detail: Dynamos coach, Mangombe, admits the team is not strong enough after a draw with Herentals extended their winless streak.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa appoints Elizabeth Gwaunza as Zimbabwe's first female Chief Justice
Zimbabwe.detail: Elizabeth Gwaunza has been appointed as Zimbabwe's first female Chief Justice.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe launches strategy to fix migration data gaps
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has launched a strategy aimed at addressing gaps in migration data.

Zimbabwe.headline: Trending: Mnangagwa quietly leaves Zimbabwe for Belarus amid constitutional amendment tensions
Zimbabwe.detail: Emmerson Mnangagwa reportedly left Zimbabwe quietly for Belarus this week, missing a key judiciary farewell event as political tensions rise over proposed constitutional amendments linked to extending his presidency.
